low_effect_resolved_per_100_loc_changed.title: Low Effect Resolved Per 100 Loc Changed

low_effect_resolved_per_changed_file.title: Low Effect Resolved Per Changed File

AgentObjective.description:
Declarative agent objective for drift.yaml.

Allows agents to declare their goal, out-of-scope areas,
and success criteria so drift can provide targeted feedback.

Example drift.yaml::

    agent:
      goal: "Migrate payment module to Stripe API"
      out_of_scope:
        - "legacy/"
        - "tests/fixtures/"
      success_criteria:
        - "No new AVS findings in src/billing/"

effectiveness_thresholds.description: Thresholds used for deterministic effectiveness warnings (e.g. low_effect_high_churn).

goal.description: Natural-language description of the agent's current task.
goal.title: Goal

out_of_scope.description: Glob patterns or paths the agent should not modify or analyze.
out_of_scope.title: Out Of Scope

strict_guardrails.description: When true, MCP orchestration enforces strict preconditions and blocks unsafe tool transitions with recovery hints.
strict_guardrails.title: Strict Guardrails

AgentObjective.title: AgentObjective

AttributionConfig.description:
Configuration for causal attribution enrichment (ADR-034).

When enabled, findings are enriched with git-blame provenance data
identifying the commit, author, and date that introduced the drifting code.

Example drift.yaml::

    attribution:
      enabled: true
      timeout_per_file_seconds: 3.0

cache_enabled.description: Cache blame results per file content hash.
cache_enabled.title: Cache Enabled

enabled.description: Enable git-blame-based causal attribution on findings.
enabled.title: Enabled

include_branch_hint.description: Attempt to extract branch name from merge-commit messages.
include_branch_hint.title: Include Branch Hint

max_parallel_workers.description: Thread pool size for parallel blame calls.
max_parallel_workers.title: Max Parallel Workers

timeout_per_file_seconds.description: Maximum seconds for a single git-blame subprocess call.
timeout_per_file_seconds.title: Timeout Per File Seconds

AttributionConfig.title: AttributionConfig

scope_aliases.description: Keyword → path mapping for scope resolution. Example: {payment: src/billing/, auth: src/auth/}
scope_aliases.title: Scope Aliases

CalibrationConfig.description:
Configuration for per-repo signal calibration (ADR-035).

When enabled, drift collects and uses feedback evidence to compute
project-specific signal weights via Bayesian weight calibration.

Example drift.yaml::

    calibration:
      enabled: true
      min_samples: 20
      bug_labels:
        - bug
        - regression

auto_recalibrate.description: Automatically recalibrate after each drift analyze run.
auto_recalibrate.title: Auto Recalibrate

bug_labels.description: Issue labels that identify bug reports for GitHub correlation.
bug_labels.title: Bug Labels

correlation_window_days.description: Days after a scan to look for defect-fix commits (TP evidence).
correlation_window_days.title: Correlation Window Days

decay_days.description: Days after which the calibration profile is considered stale.
decay_days.title: Decay Days

feedback_path.description: Path to the feedback JSONL file (relative to repo root).
feedback_path.title: Feedback Path

fn_boost_factor.description: How much to boost weight for signals with high false-negative rate (0.0 disables FN boosting, max 1.0).
fn_boost_factor.title: Fn Boost Factor

github_token.description: GitHub API token for issue/PR correlation (or use DRIFT_GITHUB_TOKEN env).
github_token.title: Github Token

history_dir.description: Directory for scan history snapshots (relative to repo root).
history_dir.title: History Dir

max_snapshots.description: Maximum number of scan snapshots to retain.
max_snapshots.title: Max Snapshots

min_samples.description: Minimum TP+FP observations per signal for full confidence. Below this, calibration blends toward default weights.
min_samples.title: Min Samples

shared_feedback_path.description: Optional team-shared feedback JSONL path (relative to repo root). When set, mark/calibrate commands read and write this path instead of calibration.feedback_path.
shared_feedback_path.title: Shared Feedback Path

threshold_adaptation_enabled.description: Enable adaptive threshold adjustment per signal based on feedback metrics.  Experimental — disabled by default.
threshold_adaptation_enabled.title: Threshold Adaptation Enabled

weak_fp_window_days.description: Days without a defect-fix → weak FP evidence.
weak_fp_window_days.title: Weak Fp Window Days

CalibrationConfig.title: CalibrationConfig

DeferredArea.description:
A glob-matched area marked as known technical debt (not excluded).

Unlike ``exclude``, deferred areas are still analysed.  Findings in
deferred areas are tagged ``deferred=True`` so CI gates and reports
can treat them differently.
DeferredArea.title: DeferredArea

DocImplDriftConfig.description: DIA-specific configuration for doc-implementation drift detection.
DocImplDriftConfig.title: DocImplDriftConfig

FindingContextPolicy.description: Policy for finding-context classification and triage behavior.
FindingContextPolicy.title: FindingContextPolicy

FindingContextRule.description: Classify findings by file path using glob patterns.
FindingContextRule.title: FindingContextRule

GradeBandConfig.description:
A single grade band entry for the composite drift score.

Grade bands map score ranges to letter grades and human-readable labels.
Thresholds are evaluated in order; the first entry whose ``threshold``
exceeds the score is selected.

Example::

    - threshold: 0.20
      grade: A
      label: "Excellent"
GradeBandConfig.title: GradeBandConfig

GuidedThresholds.description:
Score band thresholds for guided-mode traffic light (green / yellow / red).

These thresholds determine how the composite drift score maps to a
traffic-light status in ``drift status`` / guided mode.

green_max.description: Scores at or below this value are shown as green (healthy).
green_max.title: Green Max

yellow_max.description: Scores above green_max and at or below this value are shown as yellow (caution).
yellow_max.title: Yellow Max

GuidedThresholds.title: GuidedThresholds

typescript.description: Enable TypeScript/TSX/JS/JSX analysis. Requires drift-analyzer[typescript] (tree-sitter). Set to false to skip TS files even when tree-sitter is installed.
typescript.title: Typescript

LazyImportRule.description: Policy rule for enforcing lazy imports of selected modules.
LazyImportRule.title: LazyImportRule

PathOverride.description:
Per-path configuration overrides.

Match paths using glob patterns (e.g. ``tests/**``, ``legacy/``).
More specific patterns take precedence over broader ones.
PathOverride.title: PathOverride

PerformanceConfig.description: Worker tuning controls for analysis pipeline execution.

io_heavy_non_parser_ratio.description: When the non-parser file share exceeds this ratio, auto mode dampens workers to account for higher I/O overhead.
io_heavy_non_parser_ratio.title: Io Heavy Non Parser Ratio

large_file_ratio_threshold.description: Share of large files that triggers additional worker dampening.
large_file_ratio_threshold.title: Large File Ratio Threshold

large_file_size_bytes.description: Files above this size are considered I/O-heavy for conservative tuning.
large_file_size_bytes.title: Large File Size Bytes

load_profile.description: Auto-tuning profile. Initial rollout supports conservative only.
load_profile.title: Load Profile

max_workers.description: Upper clamp for auto-tuned worker counts.
max_workers.title: Max Workers

min_workers.description: Lower clamp for auto-tuned worker counts.
min_workers.title: Min Workers

small_repo_file_threshold.description: Repos at or below this file count are downscaled to avoid over-parallelization.
small_repo_file_threshold.title: Small Repo File Threshold

worker_strategy.description: Worker resolution strategy. 'fixed' uses CPU fallback (or env/CLI override). 'auto' applies conservative tuning based on repository size, file types, and I/O proxy load.
worker_strategy.title: Worker Strategy

PerformanceConfig.title: PerformanceConfig

PluginConfig.description:
Configuration for Drift plugins.

Plugins are discovered via Python entry_points (see drift.plugins).
This configuration section allows selectively disabling specific plugins.

Example drift.yaml::

    plugins:
      disabled:
        - my_broken_plugin

disabled.description: List of plugin entry-point names to skip at discovery time.
disabled.title: Disabled

PluginConfig.title: PluginConfig

RecommendationsConfig.description:
Configuration for the adaptive recommendation engine (ARE).

Controls outcome tracking, effort calibration, and recommendation
refinement.  All features are opt-in (``enabled: false`` by default).

Example drift.yaml::

    recommendations:
      enabled: true
      archive_after_days: 180

archive_after_days.description: Archive resolved outcomes older than this many days.
archive_after_days.title: Archive After Days

calibration_path.description: Path to the effort calibration JSON file (relative to repo root).
calibration_path.title: Calibration Path

enabled.description: Enable adaptive recommendation engine (outcome tracking + reward chain).
enabled.title: Enabled

min_calibration_samples.description: Minimum resolved outcomes per signal type before effort calibration kicks in.
min_calibration_samples.title: Min Calibration Samples

outcome_path.description: Path to the outcome JSONL file (relative to repo root).
outcome_path.title: Outcome Path

refinement_threshold.description: Recommendations with reward >= this threshold skip refinement.
refinement_threshold.title: Refinement Threshold

RecommendationsConfig.title: RecommendationsConfig

ScoringConfig.description:
Tunable scoring-engine parameters (drift.yaml → ``scoring:``)

All settings default to the validated ADR-003/ADR-041 values and are
backward-compatible — changing these knobs only affects repos that
explicitly set them.

Example drift.yaml::

    scoring:
      dampening_k: 10
      breadth_cap: 3.0
      feedback_blend_alpha: 0.3

breadth_cap.description: Ceiling for the log-based breadth multiplier applied to impact scores (ADR-041). Prevents very large related-file clusters from inflating scores unboundedly.
breadth_cap.title: Breadth Cap

dampening_k.description: Count-dampening constant for signal aggregation (ADR-041). Finding counts above this value produce a dampening factor near 1.0. Lower values increase sensitivity to high-finding signals.
dampening_k.title: Dampening K

feedback_blend_alpha.description: Blend factor for feedback-informed weight adjustment. 0.0 = pure auto-calibrate from finding distribution (default). 1.0 = weights fully driven by persisted feedback metrics. Values between 0 and 1 linearly interpolate both approaches. Effective only when ``calibration.enabled = true`` and feedback data exists at ``calibration.feedback_path``.
feedback_blend_alpha.title: Feedback Blend Alpha

grade_bands.description: Ordered list of score thresholds that map drift scores to letter grades. Each entry selects the grade when ``score < threshold``. The last entry should have a threshold above 1.0 as a catch-all.
grade_bands.title: Grade Bands

ScoringConfig.title: ScoringConfig

SignalWeights.description:
Weights for each detection signal in composite scoring.

Weights are normalised internally — they don't need to sum to 1.0,
but a warning is emitted if they deviate significantly.

Signals can be run in report-only mode by assigning weight 0.0.
This keeps detection visible while removing score impact until
precision/recall is sufficiently validated.
SignalWeights.title: SignalWeights

diff_baseline_recommend_max_changed_file.title: Diff Baseline Recommend Max Changed Files

diff_baseline_recommend_max_new_findings.title: Diff Baseline Recommend Max New Findings

diff_baseline_recommend_max_out_of_scope.title: Diff Baseline Recommend Max Out Of Scope Findings

description: Main drift configuration, loaded from drift.yaml.

agent.description: Optional agent objective declaration. Helps drift provide targeted feedback aligned with the agent's task.

extends.description: Name of a built-in preset to inherit from (e.g. 'vibe-coding', 'strict', 'fastapi', 'library', 'monorepo'). User-level fields override preset defaults.
extends.title: Extends

git_history_index_enabled.description: Enable persistent incremental git-history index under cache_dir to avoid full git-log parsing on repeated scans.
git_history_index_enabled.title: Git History Index Enabled

git_history_index_subdir.description: Subdirectory inside cache_dir used for persistent git-history index artifacts (manifest + commits jsonl).
git_history_index_subdir.title: Git History Index Subdir

guided_thresholds.description: Score band thresholds for guided-mode traffic light. When set via 'extends:', the profile's guided thresholds are applied automatically.

language.description: ISO 639-1 output language for guided mode (e.g. 'de', 'en').
language.title: Language

output_mode.description: Controls how much prescriptive guidance drift includes in API responses. 'full' (default): all repair instructions, constraints, verify plans, and tool choreography. 'mirror': diagnostic facts only — structural observations, scores, and deltas without prescribing what the agent should do.
output_mode.title: Output Mode

test_file_handling.description: Global handling for findings in test files: exclude | reduce_severity | include. When null, each signal uses its built-in default.
test_file_handling.title: Test File Handling

title: DriftConfig
